message:
test(fcp-core): pin ConnectorBundle Display + RegistryEntry serde + Hash (flywheel_connectors-huv65)

ConnectorBundle (connector_artifacts.rs:213) IS a real type with
Display impl (line 235) and serde derives. Two complementary test
files land:

- connector_bundle_display_serde.rs (basic surface): Display format
  pinned at "linux-amd64 connector bundle (manifest_toml=N bytes,
  binary=N bytes)", JSON shape with byte-array binary field, CBOR
  round-trip preserves fields + Display.
- connector_bundle_serde_extended.rs (extended pinning): Display
  generalizes across os/arch combos (linux-amd64, macos-arm64,
  windows-amd64, freebsd-x86_64), Display byte-counts match actual
  .len() across sizes (0 / 1 / 1024 / 65_536 bytes), empty
  manifest+binary still well-formed, multi-byte UTF-8 manifest
  preserved through JSON and CBOR (日本語 + emoji), distinct bundles
  produce distinct serializations on each field axis (manifest /
  binary / target).

RegistryEntry (the paired type that indexes ConnectorBundle
artifacts in the registry, line 249) also pinned:
- JSON shape with symbol_set_object_id omitted when None and
  present (as 64-char hex) when Some via skip_serializing_if
- JSON + CBOR round-trip preserves all fields including
  Some(symbol_set_object_id)
- Hash + Eq correctness: equal entries hash equally, distinct
  entries (on each axis: version / target / symbol_set) hash
  distinctly, RegistryEntry serves as HashMap key

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
